title: rhamnus oleoides subsp. lycioides
intro: Rhamnus oleoides subsp. lycioides, also known as the Lycioid Buckthorn, is a plant species belonging to the Rhamnaceae family. Traditionally, it has been valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, among other ailments, due to its high content of flavonoids and tannins, which provide its health benefits. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Rhamnus oleoides subsp. lycioides are often dried and used as a tea or infused in hot water to create a decoction, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
